channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Witkoff, Kushner to head to new Iran peace talks in Pakistan, White House says
item.description: Witkoff and Kushner will attend Iran peace talks in Pakistan. Live Updates: Witkoff, Kushner to head to new Iran peace talks in Pakistan, White House says CBS News.

item.title: Trump's envoys Witkoff and Kushner to fly to Pakistan for Iran talks
item.description: US envoys Witkoff and Kushner are flying to Pakistan for potential Iran talks. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is already in Pakistan. Iran says its Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is already in Pakistan but that there is no meeting planned between Iran and the US.

item.title: A weekend in Hoi An, Vietnam: Where old-world charm meets new discoveries
item.description: Hoi An in Vietnam retains its old-world charm with preserved shophouses and lantern-lit nights. Hoi An still charms with its well-preserved shophouses and lantern-lit nights, while newer restaurants, cafes and cultural experiences give this weekend escape fresh appeal.

item.title: US sending envoys to Pakistan, raising hopes of talks with Iran’s Araghchi
item.description: US envoys are heading to Pakistan to potentially meet Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in Islamabad. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrives in Islamabad, but Tehran yet to commit to more talks with US delegation.

item.title: Mass wedding for 300 brides and grooms in central Gaza
item.description: A charity sponsored a mass wedding for 300 couples in Deir el-Balah, Gaza. A festive moment in Gaza as a charity-sponsored mass wedding ceremony for 300 brides and grooms in Deir el-Balah.

item.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq end at records as Intel shares surge
item.description: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq reached record highs as Intel shares surged in global stock markets. Global stock markets have recently managed to recover the heavy losses they suffered at the start of the Middle East war.

item.title: Israel continues attacks on Lebanon despite extension of ceasefire
item.description: Israeli forces killed six Hezbollah fighters in southern Lebanon. This exchange of fire occurred despite an extended ceasefire. Israeli forces killed six Hezbollah fighters in an exchange of fire in the area of Bint Jbeil, southern Lebanon.

item.title: Vessels seized by the U.S. this week were part of a global 'shadow fleet'
item.description: The US seized vessels part of a global shadow fleet. NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Elisabeth Braw, senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, about shadow vessels, after the U. S. military's seizure of two Iranian-linked oil tankers.

item.title: Petro becomes first president to visit Venezuela since Maduro abduction
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro met with interim Venezuelan President Delcy Rodriguez, marking the first presidential visit since Maduro's abduction. Border security is expected to be a key topic in their discussions.

item.title: US, Iran Prepare For 2nd Round Of Talks In Pak Without Vance, Ghalibaf
item.description: The US has confirmed that two key officials will fly to Pakistan tomorrow for talks with Iran. The talks aim to address the ongoing conflict. US Confirms Talks With Iran, 2 Key Officials To Fly To Pak Tomorrow.

item.title: US appeals court rejects Trump’s ban on asylum seekers, teeing up appeal
item.description: A US appeals court rejected President Trump's ban on asylum seekers, stating it disregarded federal laws granting the right to seek asylum. The decision sets up an appeal, potentially impacting border policies.

item.title: India Is "Top-Tier Security Partner": Australian Envoy To NDTV
item.description: Australia's envoy to India described India as a "top-tier security partner" on NDTV, highlighting the significance of India in Australia's National Defence Strategy. This underscores growing strategic ties between the two nations.

item.title: Israeli attacks on Lebanon may violate international law, UN warns
item.description: The UN warned that Israeli attacks on Lebanon may violate international law. Israeli attacks on Lebanon may violate international law, UN warns Al Jazeera.

item.title: Justice Department drops investigation into Fed Chair Powell
item.description: The US Justice Department dropped its investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, removing an obstacle to Kevin Warsh's potential appointment as head of the central bank, a pick made by President Donald Trump.

item.title: Britain and Spain Reject Reported Plans by Trump to Punish Them
item.description: Britain and Spain rejected reported US plans to punish them for not supporting the war in Iran. An internal Pentagon email, reported by the Reuters news agency, suggested Washington was reviewing options to penalize the two nations for insufficiently supporting the war in Iran.

item.title: US millionaire big-game hunter dies after being crushed by elephants
item.description: American millionaire big-game hunter Ernie Dosio, 75, died in Gabon after being crushed by a group of elephants while hunting yellow-backed duiker. Dosio was a vineyard owner on a hunting expedition in Africa when the incident occurred.

item.title: 23, Including US Defence Staff, Injured In Bus Accident Near Pentagon
item.description: 23 people were injured in a bus accident near the Pentagon. The accident altered mass transit operations. 23, Including US Defence Staff, Injured In Bus Accident Near Pentagon.

item.title: Trump dispatches Witkoff, Kushner to Pakistan for talks with Iran’s FM Araghchi; Vance on ‘standby’
item.description: Trump sent Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for talks with Iran's FM Araghchi. U. S. delegation would likely arrive in Islamabad on April 25, once the Iranian delegation works out the framework of talks with the U.

item.title: Israel poses direct threat to global security: Turkish FM Fidan
item.description: Turkish FM Fidan said Israel poses a direct threat to global security. Israel poses direct threat to global security: Turkish FM Fidan TRT World.

item.title: Trump Sending 2 Key Officials To Pak For Talks With Iran: Report
item.description: US President Donald Trump is sending two key officials to Pakistan for talks with Iran, according to a report. The Iranians have not confirmed if they will meet with the US delegation, which is expected to discuss regional security and other issues.
